name: Chicken in Orange Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut chicken in half lengthwise.
2. Blend butter and mustard together.
3. Rub the mixture on the inside and outside of the chicken.
4. Place the chicken, skin side down, in a baking dish with a tight-fitting cover.
5. Preheat the oven to 375°F (190°C).
6. Combine orange juice and brown sugar in a bowl until the sugar is dissolved.
7. Pour this mixture over and around the chicken.
8. Cover and bake for 30 minutes.
9. Turn the chicken over, basting with pan juices occasionally.
10. Continue baking, uncovered, for an additional 20 minutes.
11. Remove the chicken to a heated platter and keep warm.
12. Mix cornstarch with water in a bowl.
13. Stir this mixture into the juices from the pan.
14. Cook over low heat, stirring constantly, until thickened.
15. Remove from heat, stir in vodka, and pour the sauce over the chicken.
Sylvia's comments: I'm unenthusiastic about this one, and I'm not sure why. The chicken was pretty bland, but chicken usually tastes bland to me, and the sauce was certainly flavorful. The combination of mustard and orange juice didn't do a thing for my taste buds. It also took about 1/2 hour longer to bake than the recipe called for. Next time, I think I'll leave out the mustard/butter rub, add a little pumpkin pie spice to the orange juice/brown sugar mixture, and cook the whole mess in the crockpot rather than the oven.
